explain: to arrange or order things so that they can be found or used easily and quickly; to put things into a particular arrangement or order
a group of people or things that are similar in some way
a warm-blooded animal that gives birth to its babies
an animal (such as a snake, lizard, turtle, or alligator) that has cold blood, that lays eggs, and that has a body covered with scales or hard parts
a class of living things that have common characteristics
one of the great divisions of land (such as North America, South America, Europe, Asia, Africa, Australia, or Antarctica) of the Earth
belonging to or shared by two or more people or groups
statement: His office is a mess. He needs someone to help him organize his papers.
The cars belong to the same category.
Whales are mammals; fish are not.
Do you think reptiles make good pets?
Her new classmates were really unfriendly.
My cat gave birth to a cute furry kitten.
Air bags are a standard feature in most new cars.
The giant panda has become an endangered species.
I want to travel the seven continents.
